uk: + Св. отця нашого Івана Золотоустого, архиєпископа Царгородського
saints.en: St. John Chrysostom, Archbishop of Constantinople

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи взиваю» – 6 стихир свят., Сл.: свят., І нині: богородичний-догмат за гласом «Слава». Вхід. Прокімен дня і три читання свят.: Прип. 10:6-8, 8:6-21, Муд. 4:7-15, Муд. 8:17-9:14. Стиховня – свят., зі своїми приспівами, Сл.: свят., І нині: богород. з неділ. стиховні за гласом «Слава». Тропар свят., Слава, І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом тропаря свят. Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби свят.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар свят. (2), Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних, за гласом тропаря свят. Катизма чергова з сідальними свят., також полієлей з величаннями. Степенна 4-го гласу, перший антифон. Прокімен, Єв. – Ів. 35 зач.; 10, 1-9. Пс. 50, і стихира свят. Канон Богородиці і свят. Катавасія: «Відкрию уста мої». По 9-ій – світильний свят. Хвалитні стихири свят. – 4. Сл.: свят., І нині: богород. за гласом наславника. По великому славослов'ї з трисвятим – тропар свят., Слава, І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом тропаря свят. Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби свят.
Часи: тропар і кондак свят.
Літургія: все – тільки свят. Апостол і євангелія: Ряд.: Ап. – 2 Сол. 277 зач.; 3, 6-18. Єв. – Лк. 73 зач.; 13, 31-35. Св.: Ап. – Євр. 318 зач.; 7, 26 – 8, 2. Єв. – Ів. 36 зач.; 10, 9-16.

troparion_en.text: Grace shining forth from your mouth like a beacon has illumined the inhabited earth; /  it has disclosed to the world treasures which wealth cannot buy;  / and has set forth for us the height of humility. /  Instructing us by your words, O Father John Chrysostom, /  intercede with the Word, Christ God, that our souls may be saved.

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “When You had fulfilled Your dispensation for our sakes”1...)

You received divine grace from Heaven, /  and by your lips you teach all men to worship the one God in Trinity,  / O most blessed Venerable John Chrysostom.  / We praise you, as is meet,  / for you are a guide, making divine things clear.

matched_oca_saint: Saint John Chrysostom, Archbishop of Constantinople

oca_saints.name: Saint John Chrysostom, Archbishop of Constantinople

troparion.text: Grace shining forth from your mouth like a beacon has illumined the inhabited earth; /  it has disclosed to the world treasures which wealth cannot buy;  / and has set forth for us the height of humility. /  Instructing us by your words, O Father John Chrysostom, /  intercede with the Word, Christ God, that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “When You had fulfilled Your dispensation for our sakes”1...)

You received divine grace from Heaven, /  and by your lips you teach all men to worship the one God in Trinity,  / O most blessed Venerable John Chrysostom.  / We praise you, as is meet,  / for you are a guide, making divine things clear.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Nikephoros, Antoninus, and Germanus of Caesarea, in Palestine

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Nicephorus, Antoninus, and Germanus, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyr Manetha of Cæsarea in Palestine

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Manetha, O Lord, / through her sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, she laid low her adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through her intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Manetha;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Monastic Martyr Damascene of the Lavra

troparion.text: The image of God was truly preserved in you, O Father, / for you took up the Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal. / Therefore your spirit, venerable Damascene, rejoices with the angels.

kontakion.text: As a venerable and proven ascetic, as an honorable and renowned martyr,  / and as a model of the desert life, O most exalted Damascene, /  we sing hymns of praise to you,  / for you have trampled upon serpents.

oca_saints.name: Stockholm Icon of the Mother of God

troparion.text: Let us sinners and wretched people now run most earnestly to the Theotokos,  /  and let us fall down in repentance, crying out from the depths of our souls:   /  “O Sovereign Lady, have compassion on us,  /  help us, and come quickly, for we are perishing from a multitude of transgressions.  /  Do not turn your servants away empty-handed, for we have you as our only hope.”

kontakion.text: We have no other help, we have no other hope, but you, O Sovereign Lady.  / Help us, for in you do we hope,  / and of you do we boast, for we are your servants.  /  Let us not be put to shame.
